title: pleopeltis aquatica
intro: Pleopeltis aquatica, also known as the water spikenard or water polypody, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pleopeltis aquatica include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and rhizomes are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health conditions.
